title: Locked Out Of EUV, Huawei Says Lithography Was Never The Point — Aninews ep 15
desc: Daily AI news satire. Huawei announced the Tau (τ) Scaling Law at IEEE ISCAS Shanghai on May 25, 2026 — measuring chip progress by signal travel time instead of transistor size, with 381 chips already produced and a 1.4nm-equivalent density target by 2031, without using a 1.4nm process.

characters.name: hetingbo
characters.description: The Chip Queen — caricature of Huawei executive He Tingbo, president of the company's semiconductor business and the keynote speaker who introduced the Tau Scaling Law at IEEE ISCAS Shanghai. Mid-fifties East Asian woman, chin-length dark bob, dark-rimmed rectangular glasses, navy single-breasted blazer over a navy-and-white striped knit top, navy trousers, dark heels. Composed commanding presence. New locked-ref this episode, generated from the official Huawei executive-bio portraits.

beats.name: chip-queen-cold-open
beats.narration: On Monday, at an IEEE conference in Shanghai, Huawei announced that the rules of semiconductor progress have been changed.

beats.name: the-old-way-is-slow
beats.narration: For sixty years, chip progress was measured by how small you could make a transistor.

beats.name: the-denied-fortress
beats.narration: Huawei, which cannot buy the machines required to make smaller transistors, has proposed an alternative.

beats.name: shanghai-lab
beats.narration: Progress, the company explained, should henceforth be measured by how fast a signal can travel across the chip. A number the company calls tau.

beats.name: the-fold
beats.narration: To support this claim, executive He Tingbo presented a new architecture called LogicFolding, which reportedly shortens critical wiring paths without requiring any new lithography.

beats.name: the-speed-test
beats.narration: Huawei also revealed that it has already produced three hundred and eighty-one chips.

beats.name: kirin-phone-unveil
beats.narration: The first commercial product is a Kirin chip launching this fall.

beats.name: vault-of-portals
beats.narration: By twenty thirty-one, Huawei projects its chips will achieve a transistor density equivalent to a one-point-four nanometer process. Without ever using a one-point-four nanometer process.

beats.name: cleanroom-forge
beats.narration: Industry analysts described the announcement as strategically convenient.

beats.name: the-shanghai-pulse
beats.narration: One observer called the move, quote, shrewd corporate strategy.

beats.name: folded-city-2031
beats.narration: The company added that no single company can independently find all the answers.

beats.name: fold-collapse
beats.narration: Especially, the company did not add, the answers behind the export restrictions.

PR Newswire — HUAWEI Introduces Tau Scaling Law for Future Chips
CGTN — Huawei unveils 'Tau Scaling Law' to hit 1.4-nanometer equivalent chip density by 2031
TrendForce — Huawei Unveils New Semiconductor Principle – Tau (τ) Scaling Law

transcript: On Monday, at an IEEE conference in Shanghai, Huawei announced that the rules of semiconductor progress have been changed. For sixty years, chip progress was measured by how small you could make a transistor. Huawei, which cannot buy the machines required to make smaller transistors, has proposed an alternative. Progress, the company explained, should henceforth be measured by how fast a signal can travel across the chip. A number the company calls tau. To support this claim, executive He Tingbo presented a new architecture called LogicFolding, which reportedly shortens critical wiring paths without requiring any new lithography. Huawei also revealed that it has already produced three hundred and eighty-one chips. The first commercial product is a Kirin chip launching this fall. By twenty thirty-one, Huawei projects its chips will achieve a transistor density equivalent to a one-point-four nanometer process. Without ever using a one-point-four nanometer process. Industry analysts described the announcement as strategically convenient. One observer called the move, quote, shrewd corporate strategy. The company added that no single company can independently find all the answers. Especially, the company did not add, the answers behind the export restrictions.

writing.author: Claude
writing.role: script · beat plan · narration · sources audit

showcase.notes: Aninews ep 15. First 720p episode (eps 2–14 ran at 480p). Kling 3.0 vs Seedance Fast 720p bake-off on beats 1–3; Seedance won on detail density and was used for all 12 beats. New locked-character ref: He Tingbo (Huawei's 'Chip Queen'). Three new rules.md clauses came out of this episode — no mirror-tiled backgrounds, no held slow-mo aftermath beats, no bird's-eye flyover-over-a-location beats; plus restate the 2D-anime style constraint at per-beat level on landscape-heavy beats.
